title: Ribollita Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup

seo.title: Ribollita Recipe: Authentic Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup
seo.metaDescription: Learn to make authentic Ribollita, the hearty Tuscan twice-boiled bread and vegetable soup. This traditional Italian Ribollita recipe is perfect for a cozy meal.
seo.h1: Authentic Ribollita Recipe: Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup
h2Headings: What is Ribollita Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup?
Pro Tips for the Best Results
seo.imageAlt: A rustic bowl of authentic Ribollita Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup with crusty bread.
seo.imageCaption: Enjoy a taste of Tuscany with this comforting and traditional Ribollita soup recipe.

description: Ribollita is a quintessential Tuscan peasant soup, renowned for its hearty, rustic character. This traditional twice-boiled soup combines stale bread, cannellini beans, and a medley of seasonal vegetables to create a truly comforting and flavorful meal. It embodies the essence of cucina povera, transforming simple ingredients into a rich and satisfying dish perfect for any season.

ingredients.notes: stale, crusty bread, torn into pieces

instructions.title: Prepare the Soffritto
instructions.text: In a large heavy-bottomed pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion, carrots, and celery. Cook gently, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are softened and translucent, about 8-10 minutes. This is your soffritto, the flavor base.

instructions.title: Add Aromatics and Tomatoes
instructions.text: Stir in the minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant. Be careful not to burn the garlic. Add the diced tomatoes with their juices and the sprig of rosemary if using. Let it simmer for 5 minutes, allowing the flavors to meld.

instructions.title: Incorporate Beans and Broth
instructions.text: Add both cans of cannellini beans, one whole and one mashed, to the pot. The mashed beans will help thicken the soup. Pour in the vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it simmer for at least 15 minutes to allow the flavors to deepen.

instructions.title: Add Greens and Season
instructions.text: Stir in the chopped cavolo nero and shredded green cabbage. Continue to simmer, uncovered, for another 10-15 minutes, or until the greens are tender. Season generously with salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste.

instructions.title: The First Boil and Bread Layer
instructions.text: Remove the rosemary sprig. Layer about half of the torn stale bread pieces into the bottom of an oven-safe casserole dish or back into the cleaned pot if not serving directly. Ladle half of the hot soup over the bread, ensuring it is well soaked. Repeat with the remaining bread and soup, creating layers.

instructions.title: The Second Boil (Ribollita)
instructions.text: Preheat your oven to 375F (190C). Place the casserole dish in the oven and bake for 20-25 minutes, or until the top is slightly crisp and golden, and the soup is bubbling. Alternatively, you can let it rest covered for at least 30 minutes, allowing the bread to fully absorb the liquid, then gently reheat on the stovetop. This second cooking is what gives Ribollita its name and characteristic texture.

instructions.title: Serve with a Drizzle
instructions.text: Remove from the oven or stovetop and let it rest for a few minutes before serving. Drizzle generously with fresh extra virgin olive oil and a sprinkle of black pepper. Serve hot, perhaps with a side of more crusty bread.

tips: For an even richer flavor, prepare the soup a day ahead. Ribollita truly shines on the second day after the flavors have had more time to meld.
Do not skip the stale bread. Fresh bread will turn mushy. If you dont have truly stale bread, you can lightly toast fresh bread slices in the oven to dry them out.
Authentic Ribollita often uses cavolo nero, a type of Tuscan kale. If unavailable, regular kale or collard greens can be used as a substitute, but the flavor profile will be slightly different.
article: Ribollita Tuscan Twice Boiled Soup is a beloved Italian Soups and Stews dish enjoyed across Italian. This authentic recipe brings traditional flavors to your kitchen.
